key_insight: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep learning model can use weld-pool images and process parameters to predict penetration state, penetration depth, and weld cross-section morphology during laser welding.
engineering_relevance: This enables real-time in-situ quality monitoring and could reduce defects by supporting closed-loop control in laser penetration welding.
method: Captured top weld-pool images with a CMOS camera during welding.
Combined CNN-based spatial feature extraction with state-space temporal modeling and welding parameters.
Built a robust dataset and trained a multi-task predictor for penetration state, depth, and seam morphology.
evidence_signal: On the test set, the model achieved 99.35% penetration-state accuracy, 1.79 mm penetration-depth error, and 95.65% cross-section reconstruction accuracy.
watch_item: Performance may depend on consistent camera setup, image quality, and process conditions, so generalization to new weld configurations should be validated carefully.
paper_title: A multi-task spatiotemporal deep neural network for predicting penetration depth and morphology in laser welding
